genetic_lineage.overview: Mencía is indigenous to northwestern Spain. DNA analysis has confirmed it is identical to Portuguese Jaen, though the Spanish name is more widely used internationally.

parents.name: Unknown
parents.notes: No parentage established. Indigenous to Galicia/León border region.

genetic_lineage.genetic_significance: DNA confirmation that Mencía equals Portuguese Jaen resolves historical confusion and suggests the variety spread across the Spanish-Portuguese border region. The variety is genetically distinct from Cabernet Franc despite occasional flavor similarities.

clones.name: Bierzo Selection
clones.characteristics: Traditional selections from old Bierzo vineyards. Intense minerality with elegant tannins.

clones.name: Corullón Selection
clones.characteristics: Selections from the prestigious Corullón village. Maximum concentration and complexity.

clones.name: Ribeira Sacra Selection
clones.characteristics: Selections from steep terraced vineyards. Bright acidity with aromatic intensity.

clones.name: Clone MC-1
clones.characteristics: Recently certified clone with good color and aromatic development.

terroir.effect: The defining soil of Bierzo's best sites. Heat-retentive slate promotes ripening while contributing mineral character and elegant tannins.

wine_character.aromatics: Red fruit, violets, slate mineral, herbs

terroir.effect: Found in parts of Ribeira Sacra and Valdeorras. Produces more aromatic, lighter wines with pronounced floral notes.
Common in lower Bierzo sites. Produces fuller wines with softer tannins.
Related to slate, found on steep slopes. Excellent drainage and mineral expression.

susceptibilities.notes: Can be affected in humid Atlantic conditions.

resistances.notes: Thrives in the cool Atlantic-influenced climate of northwestern Spain.
Well-adapted to heroic viticulture on terraced hillsides.

disease_profile.general_notes: Mencía is an early-to-mid ripening variety that produces aromatic, medium-bodied wines often compared to Pinot Noir or Cabernet Franc. The variety's revival from near-obscurity mirrors that of Godello. Old-vine examples from steep slate slopes produce the most celebrated wines.

regional_expressions.style_summary: Spain's most exciting red wine region for terroir-driven wines. The combination of old vines, steep slate slopes, and skilled winemakers produces elegant, mineral reds that have earned international acclaim. Village and single-vineyard bottlings reveal remarkable site expression.

typical_character.aromatics: Red cherry, violets, slate mineral, herbs, graphite

regional_expressions.style_summary: Dramatic terraced vineyards along the Sil and Miño river canyons, sometimes called 'heroic viticulture' due to the extreme slopes. Produces lighter, more aromatic Mencía with bright acidity and pronounced minerality.
Better known for Godello whites but produces increasingly impressive Mencía. The continental influence creates riper, fuller wines than Ribeira Sacra.

typical_character.aromatics: Red and dark fruit, herbs, mineral
